title.en: I'm speechless! It's breathtaking! 말이 안 나와요! 기가 막혀!

description.en: Moments when you're so surprised or shocked that you're speechless! Learn the two sides of "Mal-mun-i mak-hi-da" and "Gi-ga mak-hi-da".
description.ko: 너무 놀랍거나 황당해서 말이 안 나오는 순간! "말문이 막히다"와 "기가 막히다"의 두 가지 얼굴을 함께 알아봅니다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world. Welcome back to Talkori. I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 안녕하세요 여러분~! 오늘도 여러분의 리액션을 책임질 미나입니다.
content.translation: Hello everyone~! This is Mina. here to take care of your reactions again today.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. I went to Bukhansan Mountain last weekend. And the view from the top was..., just wow. I couldn't say anything.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 와~ "말문이 막히다" 라고 하죠!
content.translation: Wow~ we say 'Mal-mun-i mak-hi-da'!

insight.title: 말문이 막히다
insight.description: Literally 'The gate of words is blocked'
insight.usage_tip: Used when you are so overwhelmed by emotion or shock that you can't speak.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 정말 너무 아름다워서 "할 말을 잃다" 라는 표현도 써요.
content.translation: It's so beautiful that we also use the expression 'Hal mareul il-ta'.

insight.title: 할 말을 잃다
insight.usage_tip: Used when you are rendered speechless by a stunning or shocking situation.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Exactly. I was just standing there like a statue.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그런데 존 씨..., 너무 황당해서 말이 안 나올 때도 있잖아요.
content.translation: But John..., there are also times when you're speechless because something is so absurd.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Oh right. Like when my cat eats my homework.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 하하하! 그럴 땐 정말 "기가 막히다" 라고 해요!
content.translation: Hahaha! In that case. We really say 'Gi-ga mak-hi-da'!

content.speaker: John
content.text: "Gi-ga mak-hi-da". I've heard that one before.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Does it only mean something bad.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 아니요~! 아주 좋은 뜻도 있어요.
content.translation: No~! It has a very good meaning too.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 음식이 진짜 맛있을 때도 "와~ 맛이 기가 막히다!" 라고 하거든요.
content.translation: When food is really delicious. We say. 'Wow~ the taste is Gi-ga mak-hi-da!'

insight.title: 기가 막히다
insight.description: To be breathless / To be amazing
insight.usage_tip: It can mean both 'absurdly bad' or 'stunningly good' depending on the tone.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "맛이 기가 막히다~!"
content.translation: The taste is breathtaking~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: So it depends on the context and my face.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! 웃으면서 말하면 감탄사. 인상을 쓰면서 말하면 황당함!
content.translation: Correct! If you say it with a smile. It's admiration. If you frown. It's absurdity!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Got it. And what was the first one again. "Mal-mun-i mak-hi-da".

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 네. 이건 주로 너무 놀라거나 당황해서 "말이 안 나오다" 라는 상황에 딱이에요.
content.translation: Yes. This is perfect for when you are so surprised or embarrassed that 'words don't come out'.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "말문이 막히다...,"
content.translation: To be speechless...,

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's try a situation. Imagine I just showed you a magic trick. I turned your phone into a real piece of chocolate.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 헉! "이게 뭐야~!"
content.translation: Huh! What is this~!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 제 휴대폰이 초콜릿이 됐다구요?
content.translation: You're saying my phone became chocolate?

content.speaker: John
content.text: Yes. And it's 100 percent dark chocolate. Try a bite.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 와..., 진짜 "말문이 막히다" 정말!
content.translation: Wow..., I'm really speechless. Honestly!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Is it amazing. Or is it absurd.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 당연히 "기가 막히다" 이 말이죠!
content.translation: Of course. I mean 'Gi-ga mak-hi-da'!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 제 휴대폰 어떡할 거예요~!
content.translation: What are you going to do about my phone~!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Just kidding. Your phone is right here in my pocket.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 에이~ 깜짝이야! 존 씨 마술 실력이 "기가 막히다" 정말!
content.translation: Hey~ you scared me! Your magic skills are really breathtaking. Honestly!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Wow. Today we learned how to be speechless in Korean.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 감정이 너무 커서 말이 안 나올 때. 오늘 배운 표현들을 꼭 기억하세요!
content.translation: When your emotions are so big that words won't come out. Make sure to remember the expressions we learned today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review the five reactions.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number one. "말문이 막히다"
content.translation: To be speechless / Breathless

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number two. "할 말을 잃다"
content.translation: To lose one's words / speechless

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number three. "말이 안 나오다"
content.translation: Words don't come out

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number four. "기가 막히다"
content.translation: To be breathtaking / To be absurd

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number five. "기가 막히게 맛있다!"
content.translation: It's breathtakingly delicious!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 정말 멋진 풍경을 보면 외쳐보세요. "우와~ 기가 막히다!"
content.translation: Everyone. If you see a wonderful view. Try shouting! 'Wow~ it's breathtaking!'

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's it for today's Talkori. See you next time.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 놀라서 말문이 막혔어요. (I was so surprised that I couldn't speak.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be blocked. To be clogged
vocabulary.example: 길이 너무 막혀요. (The road is so congested.)

vocabulary.meaning: To be breathtaking. To be absurd
vocabulary.example: 경치가 기가 막히네요. (The view is breathtaking.)
마술을 보여줄게요. (I'll show you a magic trick.)
풍경이 정말 아름다워요. (The scenery is truly beautiful.)
